hypothesis: An adaptive hybrid batching algorithm that begins training with dynamic batching and switches to static-64 once the gradient-update recompilation frequency falls below one per 100,000 steps will reduce mean combined training time by at least 20% relative to static-64 alone on heavy-tailed graph-size datasets (e.g., AFLOW) for models with >1M parameters at batch sizes ≥64 while producing no statistically significant difference in final test RMSE (p>0.1).
